danger_signals.visual_form: hostile alien enemies appearing in stage scrolls
danger_signals.meaning: active threat to player; indicates combat zone

danger_signals.visual_form: player ship destroyed on one hit (without shield)
danger_signals.meaning: heightened danger state; shield absent or depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: weapon upgrades and helper ships disappear visually after life loss
danger_signals.meaning: progression reset; return to weakened baseline state

danger_signals.visual_form: energy shield visual indicator (inferred) sustaining three hits before breaking
danger_signals.meaning: player approaching vulnerable state; limited protection remaining

safety_signals.visual_form: weapon upgrades and helper ships distributed throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: momentary safety window; player gains temporary offensive/defensive advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: smaller ships following player, copying attacks
safety_signals.meaning: augmented firepower and presence; reduced isolation in combat

safety_signals.visual_form: shield pickup item granting three-hit protection
jet form: five large forward blasts; mech form: flame engulfment + immunity frame
safety_signals.meaning: player agency to clear threats or gain temporary invincibility; controlled reset moment

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: transformable fighter-mecha prototype (jet/humanoid mech dual forms)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player ship follows horizontal/vertical scrolling; mech form broader firing angles; jet form streamlined forward focus

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: generic hostile aliens (design details not documented)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: appear throughout scrolling stages; positioned as environmental threats to humanity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: alien enemies populate scrolling stages; protagonist required to pilot transformable mecha
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must transition between jet and mech forms to adapt combat approach; enemies attack in waves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: weapon upgrades visible; helper ships follow in formation (mech) or snakelike pattern (jet)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: jet form: faster horizontal flight, forward-intensive fire; mech form: faster vertical speed, wider angles, homing missiles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: basic shot only; no helper ships; no shield indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: single-hit destruction; reduced offensive output; slower maneuverability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: mecha shifts between humanoid and fighter-jet appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: weapon change, movement pattern change, helper ship positioning change, tactical implications (homing vs. direct fire)

environmental_storytelling.example: player ship is prototype transformable fighter-mecha left from protagonist Zana Keene's grandfather; inheritance establishes motivation and legacy narrative
alien attack on humankind is the core narrative driver; stages represent battle zones against invading force
game contains references to Gundam and Macross; mecha design language signals anime/sci-fi world context without explicit dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: shoots forward intensely; flies faster horizontally; helper ships follow in snakelike pattern
wider shooting angle (sometimes backwards with upgrades); faster vertical speed; helper ships in fixed formation; missiles home on enemies
fires five large blasts forward; limited uses (Stock mode) or charged usage (Charge mode)
engulfs in flame; grants temporary invulnerability; limited or charged based on mode
populate stages in waves; attack player across scrolling environment; threat intensifies without power-ups

notes: Arrow Flash communicates world-state primarily through form-switching mechanics and power-up availability rather than explicit visual narrative. The transformable mecha serves dual purpose: mechanical gameplay differentiation and faction identity signal (humanity's technological response to alien threat). Single-hit vulnerability and power-up loss on death create tension cycles visually; the Arrow Flash ability provides controlled reset moments. Environmental storytelling relies on mecha design language (Gundam/Macross references) and premise framing (alien invasion context) rather than detailed stage scenery documentation in available sources. Limited data on actual enemy visual design, color palette, or stage environmental details prevents full manifestation layer extraction.
